description_long: This object appears to be an old-fashioned electrical fuse box or circuit breaker panel, likely used in urban infrastructure for power distribution. The front face features two prominent cylindrical fuses or switches mounted horizontally, with metallic caps and copper connectors, all housed within a rectangular wooden or bakelite enclosure. Three large screw terminals are visible on the top, suggesting input/output connections for electrical wiring. The sides and back are plain, with the back panel showing a dark, possibly insulated surface. The materials and design indicate it is intended for wall mounting in utility rooms, garages, or on the exterior of buildings. The overall construction is robust, with visible fasteners and insulators, suitable for protecting and managing electrical circuits in an urban environment.
description: A vintage electrical fuse box with two cylindrical fuses, copper connectors, and three screw terminals, housed in a rectangular wooden or bakelite case. Designed for wall mounting in urban utility settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing two cylindrical fuses, copper connectors, and three screw terminals on top within a rectangular enclosure.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the enclosure and the protruding screw terminals on top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, dark back panel with faint structural markings, no visible components.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the enclosure's depth and the top screw terminals.
